steps.title: What Forum is
steps.narration: Forum is where many agents work together and every move is written down. It routes a request to the right lane and keeps a causal ledger of what actually happened.
steps.command: echo forum - accountable multi-agent orchestration with a replayable ledger
steps.stdout: forum - accountable multi-agent orchestration with a replayable ledger
steps.output: forum - accountable multi-agent orchestration with a replayable ledger

steps.title: The capability surface
steps.narration: Look at the range: route work, import an external agent trace into a verifiable ledger, grade a run with checks that can actually fail, and mine a session into gradable data. It is a flight recorder for agent teams.
steps.stdout:
usage: forum [-h] [--version]
             {status,doctor,demo,humanize,route,import-trace,grade,export-gradable,mine,submit,serve,mcp,context,runtime,ledger,gate,campaign,bench,bench-deep-verify}
             ...

Forum: accountable multi-agent orchestration.

positional arguments:
  {status,doctor,demo,humanize,route,import-trace,grade,export-gradable,mine,submit,serve,mcp,context,runtime,ledger,gate,campaign,bench,bench-deep-verify}
    status              emit Forum's Project Telos operator-spine status
    doctor              check Forum's operator-spine readiness
    demo                show Forum's operator-spine demo command
    humanize            clarify model or agent prose without adding facts
    route               route a request to a capability lane (no model needed)
    import-trace        fold an external agent trace
                        (LangSmith/OTel/AgentOps/generic JSON) into a
                        verifiable, replayable ledger — a flight recorder that
                        refutes its own tampering
    grade               grade a run's ledger — an outcome signal that CAN fail
                        and counts only independent checks (a producer cannot
                        grade itself)
    export-gradable     export a run's ledger as one forum.gradable-
                        trajectory/1 datum (prompt + trajectory + a can-fail
                        grade + off-forum re-derivation inputs)
    mine                one command: fold ANY framework's trace into a
                        verifiable ledger, grade it, and append it as
                        witnessed gradable RL data
    submit              plan and answer a request, witnessed
    serve               run the HTTP daemon
    mcp                 run the MCP (stdio) server
    context             inspect and preflight context pressure
    runtime             inspect runtime executor policy
    ledger              inspect the ledger
    gate                list and resolve human-in-the-loop approval gates
    campaign            declare, inspect, and run a witnessed multi-project
                        campaign
    bench               compare two ledgers (A/B) by their summaries
    bench-deep-verify   measure ledger verify(deep=True) scaling across
                        payload and storage variables

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit
steps.output:
usage: forum [-h] [--version]
             {status,doctor,demo,humanize,route,import-trace,grade,export-gradable,mine,submit,serve,mcp,context,runtime,ledger,gate,campaign,bench,bench-deep-verify}
             ...

Forum: accountable multi-agent orchestration.

    status              emit Forum's Project Telos operator-spine status
    doctor              check Forum's operator-spine readiness
    demo                show Forum's operator-spine demo command
    humanize            clarify model or agent prose without adding facts
    route               route a request to a capability lane (no model needed)
    import-trace        fold an external agent trace
                        (LangSmith/OTel/AgentOps/generic JSON) into a
                        verifiable, replayable ledger — a flight recorder that
                        refutes its own tampering
    grade               grade a run's ledger — an outcome signal that CAN fail
                        and counts only independent checks (a producer cannot
                        grade itself)
    export-gradable     export a run's ledger as one forum.gradable-
                        trajectory/1 datum (prompt + trajectory + a can-fail
                        grade + off-forum re-derivation inputs)
    mine                one command: fold ANY framework's trace into a
                        verifiable ledger, grade it, and append it as
                        witnessed gradable RL data
    submit              plan and answer a request, witnessed
    serve               run the HTTP daemon
    mcp                 run the MCP (stdio) server
    context             inspect and preflight context pressure
    runtime             inspect runtime executor policy
    ledger              inspect the ledger
    gate                list and resolve human-in-the-loop approval gates
    campaign            declare, inspect, and run a witnessed multi-project
                        campaign
    bench               compare two ledgers (A/B) by their summaries
    bench-deep-verify   measure ledger verify(deep=True) scaling across
                        payload and storage variables

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --version             show program's version number and exit

steps.title: A status receipt
steps.narration: Forum reports its own readiness as a receipt. The orchestrator that grades everyone else is honest enough to grade itself first, and that is exactly the property you want at the center of a swarm.
steps.stdout:
status=MATCH tool=forum command=status
next: crucible assess - verify the routed claim before public use
steps.output:
status=MATCH tool=forum command=status
next: crucible assess - verify the routed claim before public use
